publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-11-20 23:59:14 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Aric J. Lasher (1961-2024) earned a Bachelors of Architecture from Cornell University in 1984. The Aric Lasher papers (1981-2016) contains architectural drawings, concept sketches, blueprints, and other works by Lasher, including production work for feature films and building concepts and proposals for universities and municipalities.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Aric J. Lasher was born on August 29, 1961, in Detroit, Michigan. He earned a Bachelors of Architecture from Cornell University in 1984, followed by a Masters of Fine Arts in Film from the University of Southern California in 1995. Lasher practiced architecture in Lucerne, Switzerland, from 1985 to 1986, and in Chicago from 1986 to 1993. In Los Angeles, he worked as a lead set designer for multiple Hollywood feature films from 1995 to 2005, including Pearl Harbor (2001), Practical Magic (1998), and Stuart Little (1999). He returned to Chicago and worked at Hammond Beeby Rupert Aigne (HBRA) from 2005 to 2010 before succeeding Thomas H. Beeby as President and Director of Design in 2011 through 2022. In 2016, he became a fellow of the America Institute of Architects (FAIA). He died on June 6, 2024, in Chicago, Illinois.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Aric Lasher papers (1981-2016) contains architectural drawings, concept sketches, blueprints, and other works of architect Aric Lasher. The collection contains production work for the sets of feature films and building concepts and proposals for several universities and municipalities. The collection also contains a copy of Lasher's undergraduate thesis.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Aric Lasher papers, #8878. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 copies of drawings 11 x 19"; 5 copies of drawings 15 x 21.5"; 3 copies of drawings 21 x 30"; 13 graphite on vellum drawings 30 x 42"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 Photographic copies: Cranbrook thesis: drawings 13.5 x 11"; 5 Photographic copies: Study for swimming pool Lucca, Italy 1983; 2 Photographic copies: Samuel Freeman House Study 1983; 4 Photographic copies: House, Ithaca, NY 1983; 2 Photographic copies: Housing, Ithaca, NY 1981; 1 Photographic copy: Summer program, Europe (Farnese) 1982; 1 Photographic copy: Vernacular House, Isola San Guilio, Italy 1982?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound copy of Lasher's thesis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound Proposal: Site Evaluation; 5 color prints of digitally painted drawings 11 x 17"

scopecontent.p: Proposal packet 11 x 17"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 copies of drawings 8.5 x 11"; 2 yellow Trace drawings 11 x 17"; 5 graphite on vellum drawings 11 x 17"; 2 graphite on vellum drawings 30 x 42"

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous Perkins and Will projects with Ralph Johnson and 2 dozen miscellaneous copies and printouts of different sizes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14 color prints of digitally painted drawings 11 x 17"

did.unittitle: Rice University: James A Baker Institute Unbuilt Proposal
